summary: Matched most of src/melee/mn/mnstagesel.c, leaving mnStageSel_8025A998 still as a nonmatching stub per the PR body. The patch replaced many mnstagesel placeholder declarations with typed functions, reconstructed stage-select data/layout state, added stage randomization and cursor/selection handling code, and updated related gm/mn headers and symbols metadata. Author-reported ninja changes_all showed main/melee/mn/mnstagesel fuzzy/matched_code rising from 0.27% to 56.97%, with 12 listed functions reaching 100% fuzzy match.

key_files.notes: Main matching work: expanded from stubs to ~519 lines, defining stage list data, static globals, user-data structs, random stage selection, GObj/JObj setup, input polling, cursor hit testing, animation updates, cleanup, and stage-id return helper.
Converted many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes to typed signatures, added platform and HSD forward includes.
Removed the old partial mnStageSel_803F06D0_t declaration after the concrete stage-list struct and array moved into mnstagesel.c.
Refined mnStageSel data symbols: split mnStageSel_803F06D0 from following string data, marked float/string data types, and corrected sdata string sizes.
Typed gm_80164330 as u8 gm_80164330(u8), used by random stage availability checks.
Promoted gm_801A42F8 to public header and typed gm_801A4B60 as void gm_801A4B60(void), both used by stage-select control flow.
Removed the static declaration for gm_801A42F8 after making it externally visible.
Typed callbacks/helpers used by mnstagesel: mn_8022EAE0(HSD_GObj*) and bool mn_8022F218(void).

what_changed: Implemented mnStageSel_802599EC as random stage selection logic over NUM_STAGES 29 with a MAX_ITER 100000 fallback, availability checks through gm_80164330, cooldown-like x4 state, and linked-pair suppression for early stage entries.

Implemented mnStageSel_80259C28, fn_80259D84, and mnStageSel_80259ED8 to handle selection confirmation/randomization, GObj/JObj creation, animation setup, user-data state machines, and selection transition effects.

Implemented fn_8025A090, fn_8025A310, fn_8025A560, fn_8025A91C, and fn_8025A974 for stage icon/highlight/cursor/fog related update callbacks, including stick-driven cursor motion clamped to screen bounds and hit testing against per-stage bounding extents.

Implemented mnStageSel_8025B850 for per-frame input/control flow, including reading all pads or a selected pad via inline get_pad, trigger aggregation, stick deadzone handling, cancel/confirm transitions, and setting selected stage id.
Implemented mnStageSel_8025BB5C cleanup/cache-preload behavior and mnStageSel_8025BBD4 to return a random stage's xB stage id.
Defined mnStageSel_803F06D0 as a 30-entry concrete stage-list array with HSD_JObj pointer, state/index bytes, stage ids, hit extents, and scale fields; asserted element size 0x1C.
Introduced several static mnstagesel globals in c rather than relying on a weak partial static header declaration.
Updated public prototypes for mnstagesel and dependent gm/mn helpers to use concrete return and parameter types instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

Corrected symbol metadata for mnStageSel data: mnStageSel_803F06D0 size changed from 0x378 to 0x348 and following mnStageSel_803F0A18 string block was split out; sdata entries at 804D50A4/AC/B4/BC were marked as strings with precise sizes.
Left mnStageSel_8025A998 unmatched, explicitly noted by the PR body: 'except mnStageSel_8025A998'.
smart_moves: Separated the real mnStageSel_803F06D0 float/struct data from adjacent string data in symbols.txt, preventing the stage-list array from incorrectly absorbing mnStageSel_803F0A18.
Replaced the old partial static struct with a full local struct plus STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...[0]) == 0x1C), preserving layout evidence while making field usage matchable.
Made dependent helper prototypes concrete before calling them from the matched code, especially gm_80164330(u8), mn_8022EAE0(HSD_GObj*), mn_8022F218(void), and gm_801A4B60(void).
Moved gm_801A42F8 from a static header declaration to the public gm_1A36.h because mnstagesel needs to call it externally.
Used a small static inline get_pad(u8 i) around HSD_PadCopyStatus indexing, likely to help reproduce codegen in the pad polling block.
Kept the still-unmatched mnStageSel_8025A998 as an explicit stub rather than overclaiming full file completion.
Author supplied a local ninja changes_all excerpt because the website bot output combined this PR with a previous one, improving evidence quality for the actual matching delta.
decomp_lessons: When a data symbol size looks too large, inspect adjacent data classes: this PR split a stage-list object from following strings and changed symbol annotations accordingly.
For menu files, matching often depends on reconstructing both the runtime GObj/JObj callback graph and the static data tables that drive positioning, animation frame offsets, and stage ids.
Promoting previously static prototypes to public headers can be necessary when a newly matched translation unit proves cross-file usage.
Concrete bool/u8/void/HSD_GObj signatures are not just cleanup; they can be required for call-site matching and for avoiding placeholder ABI ambiguity.
Keep layout assertions near reconstructed anonymous/static data structs, especially when field names remain mostly x-offset based.
For input handling matches, tiny wrapper inlines around global arrays can be useful to coax the desired indexed load pattern.
Report per-PR matching results from local tooling when automated PR bot data is stale or combines multiple PRs.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based names for unmatched or unknown-purpose functions: fn_80259D84, fn_8025A090, fn_8025A310, fn_8025A560, fn_8025A91C, fn_8025A974.
Used module-prefixed public names for known stage-select entry points: mnStageSel_802599EC, mnStageSel_80259C28, mnStageSel_80259ED8, mnStageSel_8025B850, mnStageSel_8025BB5C, mnStageSel_8025BBD4.
Kept reconstructed struct fields offset-based where semantics were uncertain, e.g. x0, x4, x8, x1E_stage_id, while giving the struct a module-specific temporary name mnStageSel_804D6C90_t.
Named a small local user-data struct StageSelUserData when its use as GObj user data was clear, but retained offset-like members x0/x2/x4.
Used symbol names matching config addresses for static globals: mnStageSel_804D6C90 through mnStageSel_804D6CAF and mnStageSel_804D50A0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added explicit padding arrays in reconstructed archive/resource structs, e.g. _1[0x30] and _2[0xb0 - 0x40], to align known fields at observed offsets.
Used a dummy local stack variable such as u64 _[2], u8 _[4], or u32 unused in several functions, indicating stack/codegen shaping for matching.
Used literal floating constants with F suffix and exact frame multipliers such as 20.0F and 50.0F to reproduce animation request calculations.
Used nested ifs rather than combined boolean expressions in selection and hit-test paths where the generated branch structure likely mattered.
Introduced MAX_ITER and NUM_STAGES macros to make magic loop bounds readable while preserving constants 100000 and 29.
Used GET_JOBJ(gobj), HSD_GObjGetUserData, GObj_InitUserData, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, and HSD_ForeachAnim patterns consistently for baselib menu object callbacks.
Used exact pad deadzone thresholds +/-0x1E and trigger masks 0x1000, 0x1100, and 0x200 in control-flow code.

review_feedback.feedback: Scope caveat: all targeted mnstagesel functions were matched except mnStageSel_8025A998.
The website did not pick up changes from the last PR, so the author posted local ninja changes_all output for just this PR.
No review comments or formal review feedback were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Search for further renames or field documentation of struct stagelistinfo and mnStageSel_804D6C90_t.
Check whether mnStageSel_803F06D0 fields x8, x9, xA, and xB were later named for unlock state, animation index, availability key, and stage id.
Look for PRs that refine gm_80164330, gm_801A42F8, gm_801A4B60, mn_8022EAE0, mn_8022F218, or mn_8022F268 prototypes.
Search for other PRs splitting symbols.txt objects from adjacent string data in menu files.
